retry_reason: Failure is due to memory allocation (kzalloc returns NULL); retrying within the same context would likely also fail unless memory pressure is relieved.
contract_summary: Create-and-set wrapper: allocates a new aa_proxy structure, initializes its embedded kref to 1 (via kref_init, establishing the initial reference), and increments the reference count on the given aa_label (via aa_get_label). The caller receives a pointer to the newly allocated proxy, which carries a single reference. If allocation fails, the function returns NULL and no references are acquired. The caller is responsible for releasing the proxy reference (via a corresponding kref_put/aa_put_proxy) when done, and must also independently release the reference to the label if needed. The label reference held by the proxy will be automatically released when the proxy's refcount drops to zero.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to allocate and initialize a reference-counted proxy object, including setting its initial kref to 1 and acquiring a reference on the passed label. The only non-refcount logic is memory allocation and basic field assignment, which are integral to creating the object.
